title: limnanthes alba subsp. gracilis
intro: Limnanthes alba subsp. gracilis, also known as the white meadowfoam, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Limnanthaceae family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been employed to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations, wounds, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include soothing skin conditions, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various skin and wound-related ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds are often used to create a topical cream or oil, which can be applied directly to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
